verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF-family member synthesized as a transmembrane-anchored precursor that undergoes ectodomain shedding via ADAM/MMP proteolysis to release the mature soluble ligand. The precursor form transiently presents on the cell surface before cleavage. Although the bioactive circulating form is secreted, the gene encodes a TM isoform that is membrane-anchored during its lifecycle, qualifying it as contextual rather than secreted-only.
